title: Race-lap query ranker blended by OOF rank
summary: ROC AUC rewards ordering, and CatBoost supports groupwise ranking objectives.
rationale: ROC AUC rewards ordering, and CatBoost supports groupwise ranking objectives. In this task, the natural query is all drivers on the same Year-Race-LapNumber, where only a few cars are truly in a pit window; learning within-lap ordering should be more strategy-aligned than another global binary model.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoost ranker with GroupId = Year_Race_LapNumber using YetiRankPairwise or LambdaMart, then blend its out-of-fold ranks with the current classifier. Keep features causal and compare standalone QueryAUC-style behavior versus blended ROC AUC.
expected_effect: Adds complementary signal on pit-window ordering and pit-wave laps; most likely a small but real AUC gain in ensemble form rather than as a replacement model.
risk: Many groups will have zero positives, so the ranker may be weak standalone and can become noisy if grouping or pair generation is not handled carefully.
